For over a year, we have been consistently having this crash. We have an app in production with over 500,000 downloads on Android. We get about 100 crashes per month, resulting in a ton of very negative reviews.


For each user that experiences this issue, they install the app, try to launch it but it fails to launch, showing them a blank grey screen instead. We've contacted users to have them try to uninstall and reinstall, but no matter how many times they try, it consistently crashes on launch for that specific device.


We have tried the two main suggested potential solutions, none of which fixed the issue; we continued to consistently get 100 crashes per month after each attempted fix:
1) unobfuscating libraries in android/gradle.properties: 
android.bundle.enableUncompressedNativeLibs=false

2) changing build targets in android/app/build.gradle:
  <details>
  <summary>abiFilters changes</summary>
  
  abiFilters changes attempt A
  ```
  ...
  android {
      defaultConfig {
          ...
          abiFilters 'armeabi-v7a','arm64-v8a','x86_64'
      }
  }
  ...
  ```
  
  abiFilters changes attempt B
  ```
  ...
  android {
      buildTypes {
          release {
              ndk {
                abiFilters 'armeabi-v7a','arm64-v8a','x86_64'
              }
          }
      }
  }
  ...
  ```
  
  abiFilters changes attempt C
  ```
  ...
  android {
      defaultConfig {
          ...
          ndk {
          abiFilters "armeabi-v7a","arm64-v8a"
          }
      }
      buildTypes {
          release {
              ...
              ndk {
                  abiFilters "armeabi-v7a","arm64-v8a"
              }
          }
      }
  }
  ...
  ```
  </details>

I faced the same problem and after few hours of investigating I found a solution

in defaultConfig tag  in `build.gradle`

```
ndk
{
        abiFilters "arm64-v8a", "armeabi-v7a", "x86", "x86_64"
}
```

in android tag in` build.gradle`

```
packagingOptions
{
    jniLibs
    {
        useLegacyPackaging true
    }
}
```

gradle.properties
`android.useDeprecatedNdk=true`

In `application ` tag in Android.manifest
`android:extractNativeLibs="true"`

This is sort of a long shot, but it does seem possible that this is an instance of https://issuetracker.google.com/issues/346717090#comment2, an Android bug affecting app bundles making use of the [Play Delivery feature](https://developer.android.com/guide/playcore/feature-delivery) (which in turn is used by Flutter's [deferred components](https://docs.flutter.dev/perf/deferred-components)).
I don't think it's directly DFM or Flutter problem, but has anyone tried [Uber team](https://issuetracker.google.com/issues/346717090#comment8) workaround from @gmacall comment?

We've just [merged a change](https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/55095) to switch to using [ReLinker](https://github.com/KeepSafe/ReLinker) instead of calling `System.loadLibrary()` directly. 

This may help fix the issue in some cases (though we can't verify this without a reproduction) - any data on crash rates from apps released using a version of Flutter that includes this change would be appreciated!
